news.title:
West Asia conflict and delayed tariff hikes are squeezing Indian telecom firms financially, raising energy and network equipment costs.
Higher diesel prices, 5G capex monetization delays, and inflationary pressures are impacting Bharti Airtel, Vodafone Idea, and Reliance Jio margins.
news.summary:
West Asia conflict and delayed tariff hikes are squeezing Indian telecom firms financially, raising energy and network equipment costs.
Higher diesel prices, 5G capex monetization delays, and inflationary pressures are impacting Bharti Airtel, Vodafone Idea, and Reliance Jio margins.

news.title:
TRAI imposed a Rs 51.1 lakh penalty on Bharti Airtel for Q1 FY24 customer complaints.
Bharti Airtel plans to appeal the order under 2018 Telecom Regulations.
news.summary:
TRAI imposed a Rs 51.1 lakh penalty on Bharti Airtel for Q1 FY24 customer complaints.
Bharti Airtel plans to appeal the order under 2018 Telecom Regulations.

news.title:
Ajit Mishra of Religare Broking recommends Bharti Airtel, Hindalco, and PFC for short-term gains amid market volatility.
Bharti Airtel targets ₹1,985, Hindalco ₹1,050, and PFC ₹490; cautious optimism advised with focus on sectoral strength.
news.summary:
Ajit Mishra of Religare Broking recommends Bharti Airtel, Hindalco, and PFC for short-term gains amid market volatility.
Bharti Airtel targets ₹1,985, Hindalco ₹1,050, and PFC ₹490; cautious optimism advised with focus on sectoral strength.

news.title:
JM Financial's Q4 playbook highlights sectors like IT, autos, telecom, and metals as key growth drivers. Preferred stocks include Infosys, Maruti Suzuki, ICICI Bank, Tata Steel, and Bharti Airtel.
Cautious on consumer, pharma, and utilities; stocks like HDFC Bank, Bajaj Finance, and LTIMindtree are marked 'avoid'. Report emphasizes sector-focused stock selection.
news.summary:
JM Financial's Q4 playbook highlights sectors like IT, autos, telecom, and metals as key growth drivers. Preferred stocks include Infosys, Maruti Suzuki, ICICI Bank, Tata Steel, and Bharti Airtel.
Cautious on consumer, pharma, and utilities; stocks like HDFC Bank, Bajaj Finance, and LTIMindtree are marked 'avoid'. Report emphasizes sector-focused stock selection.

news.title:
Axis Securities forecasts moderate Q4 growth for IT Services due to macro uncertainties.
Telecom gains led by Bharti Airtel, Reliance Jio, supported by 5G and tariff hikes.
news.summary:
Axis Securities forecasts moderate Q4 growth for IT Services due to macro uncertainties.
Telecom gains led by Bharti Airtel, Reliance Jio, supported by 5G and tariff hikes.

news.title:
Brokerages focus on Titan, DLF, Hindalco, and affordable housing NBFCs, flagging rural demand risks and margin compression.
Top picks include Bharti Airtel, Tata Steel, Home First Finance, and premium consumer plays like Titan and HUL.
news.summary:
Brokerages focus on Titan, DLF, Hindalco, and affordable housing NBFCs, flagging rural demand risks and margin compression.
Top picks include Bharti Airtel, Tata Steel, Home First Finance, and premium consumer plays like Titan and HUL.

news.title:
MOFSL sees FY27 as favorable for Indian equities post FY26 underperformance caused by FII outflows and geopolitical risks.
Top stock picks include Bharti Airtel, SBI, ICICI Bank, M&M, Titan, Infosys, and TVS Motors; mid-caps outperform large-caps.
news.summary:
MOFSL sees FY27 as favorable for Indian equities post FY26 underperformance caused by FII outflows and geopolitical risks.
Top stock picks include Bharti Airtel, SBI, ICICI Bank, M&M, Titan, Infosys, and TVS Motors; mid-caps outperform large-caps.

news.title:
Bharti Airtel deployed 4,300+ new 5G sites in UP East over 12 months.
The expansion spans 48 districts, serving 34M people in urban, town, and rural areas.
news.summary:
Bharti Airtel deployed 4,300+ new 5G sites in UP East over 12 months.
The expansion spans 48 districts, serving 34M people in urban, town, and rural areas.

news.title:
Telecom operators may report muted Q4 due to fewer operating days and rising energy costs.
Steady underlying growth is likely but offset by cost pressures, say brokerage analysts.
news.summary:
Telecom operators may report muted Q4 due to fewer operating days and rising energy costs.
Steady underlying growth is likely but offset by cost pressures, say brokerage analysts.

news.title:
Six of India's top 10 firms lost ₹65k cr in market value, Airtel hit most, losing ₹29,993 cr.
Broader market struggled on global, domestic issues; Reliance Industries retained top valuation at ₹18.28 lakh cr.
news.summary:
Six of India's top 10 firms lost ₹65k cr in market value, Airtel hit most, losing ₹29,993 cr.
Broader market struggled on global, domestic issues; Reliance Industries retained top valuation at ₹18.28 lakh cr.

news.title:
Bharti Airtel received a DoT notice imposing a Rs. 1.09 lakh penalty for subscriber verification violations.
The company decided not to contest and will pay the penalty as per compliance obligations.
news.summary:
Bharti Airtel received a DoT notice imposing a Rs. 1.09 lakh penalty for subscriber verification violations.
The company decided not to contest and will pay the penalty as per compliance obligations.

news.title:
Motilal Oswal sees value as Nifty 50 trades at 17.7x P/E, a 15% discount to historical LPA. Key picks include Bharti Airtel, SBI, BEL.
Top sectors: Auto, PSU Banks, Technology; Neutral on Telecom, Cement. Midcap correction noted; largecaps preferred for investments.
news.summary:
Motilal Oswal sees value as Nifty 50 trades at 17.7x P/E, a 15% discount to historical LPA. Key picks include Bharti Airtel, SBI, BEL.
Top sectors: Auto, PSU Banks, Technology; Neutral on Telecom, Cement. Midcap correction noted; largecaps preferred for investments.

news.title:
Swiggy, Eternal, and Nykaa are projected to show strong Q4 FY26 growth, with Swiggy's GOV up 21.6% YoY and Nykaa's revenue up 26% YoY.
Bharti Airtel and Jio expect stable growth, while Vodafone Idea may see subscriber losses; 5G rollout and debt updates are key.
news.summary:
Swiggy, Eternal, and Nykaa are projected to show strong Q4 FY26 growth, with Swiggy's GOV up 21.6% YoY and Nykaa's revenue up 26% YoY.
Bharti Airtel and Jio expect stable growth, while Vodafone Idea may see subscriber losses; 5G rollout and debt updates are key.

news.title:
Kotak upgrades Bharti Airtel to 'buy' with a 26.3% upside, citing resilient wireless performance and ARPU growth.
Lower capex (FY26-28) to drive deleveraging; Bharti Airtel crossed 650M customers, stock trading 1.5% lower at ₹1,755.4.
news.summary:
Kotak upgrades Bharti Airtel to 'buy' with a 26.3% upside, citing resilient wireless performance and ARPU growth.
Lower capex (FY26-28) to drive deleveraging; Bharti Airtel crossed 650M customers, stock trading 1.5% lower at ₹1,755.4.

news.title:
Bharti Airtel is the world's 2nd largest telco with 650M customers.
It operates in India, Africa and is expanding into 5G, NBFC ventures.
news.summary:
Bharti Airtel is the world's 2nd largest telco with 650M customers.
It operates in India, Africa and is expanding into 5G, NBFC ventures.

news.title: CAG seeks Rs 8.49 crore penalty recovery from Bharti Airtel.
news.summary: CAG seeks Rs 8.49 crore penalty recovery from Bharti Airtel.

news.title:
Kotak upgrades Bharti Airtel to Buy, sets target price at Rs 2,250.
Highlights include ARPU hike, capex acceleration, and 8x FY28E EV/EBITDA valuation.
news.summary:
Kotak upgrades Bharti Airtel to Buy, sets target price at Rs 2,250.
Highlights include ARPU hike, capex acceleration, and 8x FY28E EV/EBITDA valuation.

news.title:
Bharti Airtel added 48.5 lakh wireless subscribers in Feb 2026, leading growth for 3rd straight month.
Reliance Jio gained 17.4 lakh subscribers, while BSNL and MTNL continued subscriber losses.
news.summary:
Bharti Airtel added 48.5 lakh wireless subscribers in Feb 2026, leading growth for 3rd straight month.
Reliance Jio gained 17.4 lakh subscribers, while BSNL and MTNL continued subscriber losses.

news.title:
February TRAI data shows a slowdown in mobile user additions.
Airtel, Jio, and Vodafone Idea gained subscribers despite the slowdown.
news.summary:
February TRAI data shows a slowdown in mobile user additions.
Airtel, Jio, and Vodafone Idea gained subscribers despite the slowdown.

news.title:
Bharti Airtel sees a block deal worth Rs.96.2 crore on April 1, 2026.
The deal involved one seller and one buyer.
news.summary:
Bharti Airtel sees a block deal worth Rs.96.2 crore on April 1, 2026.
